title: hieracium maculatum subsp. cruentum
intro: Hieracium maculatum subsp. cruentum, also known as spotted hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family native to Europe and Western Asia. Traditionally, spotted hawkweed has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving pain, as well as promoting wound healing and digestion. Spotted hawkweed can be used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
